behavior_summary: Provides simple inspect (U8P_GUARD7::look) text for the guard and a full conversation handler when the player 'uses' or talks to the guard.
When inspected via U8P_GUARD7::look the script checks Npc::isDead(this) and plays a dead-variant bark if dead, otherwise a living bark; the process then suspends.

U8P_GUARD7::enterFastArea is triggered when an actor enters the guard's fast-area: if the NPC is dead it clears dead/in-combat flags and teleports the guard to fixed coordinates (revive/position); if alive it calls opaque engine behavior routines to start area behaviour.

U8P_GUARD7::schedule registers/awaits a timed/scheduled engine action, and on a positive process_result() it clears the dead flag and calls an opaque post-U8P_GUARD7::schedule method — inferred to drive respawn or reinitialization.

U8P_GUARD7::use manages conversation flow: opens a conversation, evaluates world/quest flags (hydrosFree, hasHeart, pyrosFree) to optionally produce urgent warnings, handles first-meet vs repeat greeting (guard7Met and guard7Pissed), runs a menu-driven Q&A loop (removing options as they are asked), and sets guard7Met and guard7Thanked when appropriate.
notable_control_flow: U8P_GUARD7::look branches on Npc::isDead(this) to choose dead vs living bark.
U8P_GUARD7::enterFastArea branches on Npc::isDead(this): dead => Npc::clrDead + Npc::clrInCombat + Npc::teleport; alive => METHOD::func091C, suspend, METHOD::func15E7.

U8P_GUARD7::schedule initializes temporaries, calls METHOD::func143A and suspends; after suspend it tests process_result() and if true calls Npc::clrDead(this) then METHOD::func133F and suspends again.

U8P_GUARD7::use: first-meet (guard7Met unset) vs repeat greeting (guard7Met set) split; within repeat greeting there is a branch for guard7Pissed which is cleared after an annoyed reply; a random branch selects alternate repeat greetings when not pissed.
Conversation menu loop uses Ask + SuspendAssign into local1 and strcmp to dispatch choices; MenuRemove is used to remove asked options until player selects 'Bye' to exit.
open_questions: The exact behavior of opaque methods METHOD::func091C, METHOD::func15E7, METHOD::func143A and METHOD::func133F is unknown — they likely control AI/patrol, scheduling registration, and post-U8P_GUARD7::schedule respawn, but their exact side effects are not observed.
Precise semantics and coordinate interpretation for Npc::teleport(0x29,0x00,0x64,0x64,this) (map ID and position) are inferred but not proven.
Some raw/spawn calls in U8P_GUARD7::use reference FREE::func1C19 and FREE::resetRef; their gameplay effect during conversation setup/teardown is unclear.
Local temporary initial values in U8P_GUARD7::schedule (local0..local3) are numeric constants whose meaning (timing, IDs, bitmasks) is inferred but not certain.

main_function.name: U8P_GUARD7::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short description / single-line reply when the player examines or 'looks' at the guard; select a dead or living variant based on the NPC's dead flag.
entry_conditions: Player performs a 'U8P_GUARD7::look' / inspect action on the NPC
Engine routes the U8P_GUARD7::look-type process (process type 0x0208) to U8P_GUARD7::look

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD7::enterFastArea
function_map.role: fast-area entry behavior: revive/teleport dead guard or start alive-area behavior

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD7::schedule
function_map.role: scheduling/timer callback that can trigger clearing dead state and reinitialization

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD7::use
function_map.role: conversation handler: greeting, urgent warnings, menu-driven Q&A, and persistent guard flags

function_map.name: Npc::isDead
function_map.role: engine-query to test whether the NPC is flagged dead

function_map.name: Npc::clrDead
function_map.role: engine primitive to clear the NPC dead flag (revive/mark alive)

function_map.name: Npc::clrInCombat
function_map.role: engine primitive to clear in-combat state on the NPC

function_map.name: Npc::teleport
function_map.role: engine primitive to move the NPC to a map coordinate

internal_calls.purpose: Likely start or register area/AI behavior for the alive guard branch
Opaque follow-up action after a suspend in the alive branch; tied to area behaviour sequence
Register or trigger a U8P_GUARD7::schedule/timer action the process waits on
Post-U8P_GUARD7::schedule handler invoked when process_result() is true; likely reinitializes or finalizes respawn
Opaque spawn/reset interaction performed during conversation setup (raw shows spawn/free-like operation)
Reset a reference in the engine's free/spawn subsystem after the spawn-like suspend

external_calls.purpose: Decide whether to show dead-variant or living-variant bark
Clear the dead flag when reviving a dead guard on area entry
Clear in-combat state as part of revive/rehab
Teleport the guard to a map coordinate when reviving/positioning
Check the result of the suspended U8P_GUARD7::schedule call to decide follow-up actions
Open conversation context for player-NPC dialog
Provide randomness to vary repeat greeting text

reads.name: hydrosFree
reads.likely_effect: Contributes to an urgent-warning counter that can trigger an alarm/urgent bark about Titans; used by U8P_GUARD7::use

reads.name: hasHeart
reads.likely_effect: Contributes to the same urgent-warning counter and can enable special shelter/walking-dead dialogue in U8P_GUARD7::use()

reads.name: pyrosFree
reads.likely_effect: Contributes to the urgent-warning counter used by U8P_GUARD7::use()

reads.name: guard7Met
reads.likely_effect: Read to pick between first-time greeting and repeat greeting branches in U8P_GUARD7::use()

reads.name: guard7Pissed
reads.likely_effect: Read within repeat greeting branch to determine annoyed reply behavior

reads.name: devonInRule
reads.likely_effect: Read when exiting conversation to decide whether to give a special 'thanks for helping Devon' line

reads.name: guard7Thanked
reads.likely_effect: Read (negated) to avoid repeating the Devon-thanks line on exit

writes.name: guard7Met
writes.likely_effect: Set when the player first talks to this guard to record that the guard has been met

writes.name: guard7Pissed
writes.likely_effect: Cleared/zeroed after the annoyed greeting branch, resetting annoyed state

writes.name: guard7Thanked
writes.likely_effect: Set when the guard gives the Devon 'thank you' line so the thank-you is not repeated

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides simple inspect (U8P_GUARD7::look) text for the guard and a full conversation handler when the player 'uses' or talks to the guard.
